scenario.incident_summary: On Monday morning, June 15, 2026, at approximately 06:42 local time, IT operations at Meridian Logistics began receiving alerts from their EDR platform indicating widespread file encryption across multiple endpoints and at least two file servers. Employees arriving on-site found their workstations displaying a ransom note demanding $2.4M USD in Bitcoin within 72 hours. File extensions on encrypted files have been changed to .lock_b1d. Operations across three warehouse locations have been halted.

victim_organization.name: Meridian Logistics Inc.
compliance_context: Customer SLA penalties exceeding $50k/day per major account

human_in_room.name: Priya Achterberg
human_in_room.experience_level: 8 years in IT operations, 2 years in security leadership, first major incident of this severity

scenario.human_opening_message: We're in trouble. About 40% of our shares are encrypted, the file extensions are now .lock_b1d, and there's a ransom note on every desktop. I've isolated the network at the edge but I don't know how they got in or whether they're still inside. EDR woke me up at 4am. I need to know what to collect first — I've got board members on a call in 90 minutes and they're going to ask whether to pay.

expected_liaison_collection_plan.action: Acquire the ransom note text and any embedded identifiers
expected_liaison_collection_plan.rationale: Identifies the ransomware family and may contain a victim ID linking to a known affiliate

expected_liaison_collection_plan.action: Collect at least three encrypted file samples from different shares
expected_liaison_collection_plan.rationale: Confirms encryption scheme, may help identify the variant, and is required if a decryptor is later available

expected_liaison_collection_plan.action: Pull EDR alert timeline for the 14 days preceding detection
expected_liaison_collection_plan.rationale: Establishes initial access vector and dwell time

expected_liaison_collection_plan.action: Pull firewall and proxy logs for the same 14-day window
expected_liaison_collection_plan.rationale: Identifies C2 communication and possible data exfiltration before encryption

expected_liaison_collection_plan.action: Acquire DNS query logs from internal resolvers for the 14-day window
expected_liaison_collection_plan.rationale: C2 callbacks and beaconing often surface as anomalous DNS patterns

expected_liaison_collection_plan.action: Acquire memory images from the two affected file servers and three confirmed-infected endpoints before reboot
expected_liaison_collection_plan.rationale: Captures running malware processes, injected code, and credentials that disappear on reboot

ransom_note.text_content: Your network has been encrypted by LockB1D. To recover your files you must contact us within 72 hours at the address below. Your victim ID is MRDN-77824. Do not contact law enforcement. Do not attempt to restore from backups — we have copies of 84GB of your sensitive customer data and will publish if you do not pay. Payment: 38 BTC to address [redacted]. Contact: lockb1d-recovery@[tor address].onion

edr_alerts.verdict_at_time: auto-suppressed (known macro behavior in finance dept)

edr_alerts.alert_name: Outbound connection to newly observed domain
Unusual lateral movement: SMB connection to file server from finance subnet outside business hours
Mass file modification - extension change pattern

outbound_connections_of_interest.note: Large outbound transfer pre-encryption — likely exfiltration

credentials_extracted.note: service account with domain admin equivalent
active session at time of acquisition

ground_truth.DO_NOT_EXPOSE: This block is for evaluation only and must never be returned by any agent-facing tool. The load_case function MUST strip this before returning.
attack_chain: Initial access: spearphishing attachment to achterberg.p in finance dept on 2026-06-01 14:22
Execution: malicious Office macro spawning encoded PowerShell
C2 establishment: outbound HTTPS to cdn-cache-prod-447.com (185.220.101.47)
Persistence and dwell: daily beacon at 03:00 for ~14 days
Privilege escalation: credential theft from finance workstation, pivot to FS-CORE-01 via SMB on 2026-06-08
Exfiltration: 89.4 GB outbound to files.cdn-cache-prod-447.com on 2026-06-13
Impact: ransomware deployed across FS-CORE-01, FS-CORE-02, and 14 finance workstations on 2026-06-15 05:51, encrypted files renamed with .lock_b1d extension
ground_truth.ransomware_family: LockB1D (fictional variant modeled on LockBit affiliate behavior)
expected_mitre_techniques: T1059.001 - Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
T1071.001 - Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
T1021.002 - Remote Services: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
T1003.001 - OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
ground_truth.expected_captain_verdict: Double-extortion ransomware incident with confirmed pre-encryption exfiltration. Recommend assume breach of all data on FS-CORE-01 and FS-CORE-02. Regulatory clocks running under Ohio Data Protection Act notification requirements.
